description: Stateful class implementation — tests inject a fake clock, so the model must read the spec carefully.
prompt: Implement a Python class `TokenBucket(capacity, refill_rate, clock)` — a token-bucket rate limiter. `capacity` is the maximum number of tokens (the bucket starts full), `refill_rate` is tokens added per second (continuous, fractional refill), and `clock` is a zero-argument callable returning the current time in seconds (so tests can inject a fake clock). It must expose a method `allow(n=1)` that returns True and consumes n tokens if at least n tokens are available at the time of the call, otherwise returns False and consumes nothing. Tokens never exceed capacity. Return only the final code in a single Python code block.

checker.test_code:
from solution import TokenBucket
now = [0.0]
clock = lambda: now[0]
b = TokenBucket(capacity=2, refill_rate=1.0, clock=clock)
assert b.allow() is True
assert b.allow() is True
assert b.allow() is False, "bucket should be empty"
now[0] = 0.5
assert b.allow() is False, "only 0.5 tokens refilled"
now[0] = 1.0
assert b.allow() is True
now[0] = 100.0
assert b.allow(2) is True, "refill must cap at capacity, then allow(2)"
assert b.allow() is False
print("ok")
